commands.review_summary:
The PR pre-creates the Docker state, workspace, and auth-profile config mount targets as `node:node` 0700 directories before `USER node`, then updates Dockerfile unit coverage and the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. source and linked reporter evidence show the path clearly: current main only precreates `/home/node/.openclaw`, while Compose/docs use separate workspace and auth-profile config mount targets. I did not run Docker in this read-only review, so this is source-reproducible rather than locally reproduced.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed because automation cannot provide the contributor's missing live Docker named-volume proof, and the PR has a protected maintainer-handled label.

target.title: fix(docker): precreate owned named volume targets
target.body:
Summary:
- pre-create Docker named-volume mount points for workspace and auth-profile config dirs
- verify each mount point is node-owned with 0700 permissions before switching to the node user
- update Dockerfile coverage for the additional paths

Verification:
- `pnpm test src/dockerfile.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Behavior addressed:
First-run Docker named volumes mounted at `/home/node/.openclaw/workspace` and `/home/node/.config/openclaw` could start as root-owned directories, blocking the non-root `node` runtime from writing workspace or auth-profile state.

Real environment tested:
Local macOS checkout with Dockerfile unit coverage.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
`pnpm test src/dockerfile.test.ts`

Evidence after fix:
The Dockerfile now creates `/home/node/.openclaw`, `/home/node/.openclaw/workspace`, and `/home/node/.config/openclaw` with `install -d -m 0700 -o node -g node`, and the test asserts those ownership checks happen before `USER node`.

Observed result after fix:
1 Vitest file passed, 18 tests passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested:
A live Docker build/run with fresh named volumes; the patch is constrained to the image-layer mount-point ownership contract and covered by Dockerfile assertions.

Fixes #85076.
target.author: steipete
